name: sh40-celadon

metadata.name: sh40-celadon
metadata.summary: A modern TUI library taking the right lessons from the web.

documentation:
![Celadon](https://singlecolorimage.com/get/afe1af/1600x200)

## Celadon

A modern TUI library taking the right lessons from the web.

```
pip install sh40-celadon
```

![rule](https://singlecolorimage.com/get/afe1af/1600x3)

### Development Note

Since the project is in early stages a lot of optimization remains on
the table. It already runs well on most machines, but certain domains
(such as styling & layouts) might hog older computers.

This will be remedied in the future, once the related framework is in
place to allow for more generalized testing.

![rule](https://singlecolorimage.com/get/afe1af/1600x3)

### Purpose

`Celadon` is a _library_ for creating good looking and performant
UIs in the terminal. While it _is_ possible to use standalone,
you generally wanna use it through a _framework_ like
[celx](https://github.com/shade40/celx).

![rule](https://singlecolorimage.com/get/afe1af/1600x3)

### Feature highlights

#### YAML Styling

`Celadon` includes a powerful CSS-inspired engine for styling your
applications. It supports your basic `#id`, `.group` (`.class` in CSS
land) selector primitives, as well as direct (`Parent > Widget`) and
indirect hierarchal matching (`Parent *> Widget`, where `Widget` is a
part of `Parent` but not necessarily a first-degree descendant).

It replaces CSS' notion of a 'pseudoclass' with a more direct notion
of widget _state_, controlled by a state machine internal to all widgets.

```yaml
Button:
    fill_style: '@ui.primary'
    # Automatically use either white or black, based on the W3C contrast
    # guidelines
    content_style: ''

    # On hover, become a bit brighter
    /hover: # Equivalent to 'Button/hover'
        fill_style: '@ui.primary+1'

    # Become a bit taller if in the 'big' group
    .big:
        height: 3

    # If part of a Row in the 'button-row' group, fill available width.
    # '&' stands for the selector in the previous nesting layer, `Button`
    # in this case.
    Row.button-row > &:
        width: null
```

#### A great layout system

Layouts are one of the biggest difficulties in any UI environment,
especially on the web. We try to solve most of these issues by stripping
down the amount of settings _you_ need to be aware of (no more "`align-items`
or `justify-items`?") and controlling almost all layout parameters within
widget dimension settings.

A dimension can be one of three types:

- `int`: Sets a static size, i.e. `button.width = 5` will make that button
    take up 5 cells of space horizontally, regardless of its parent's size.
- `float`: Sets a relative size to the parent's size of the same dimension,
    i.e. `button.width = 0.5` will make the button be half the width of the
    parent.
- `None`: Sets a so-called `fill` height, meaning the parent will divide
    its space remaining after sizing the widgets with the previous two
    dimension types, and divide it evenly amongst the `fill` sized.

**Exhibit A: Traditional 'web' Header-Body-Footer layout**

```python
app += Page(
    Tower(
        Row(
            Text("I mimick the web"),
            Text("For it cannot mimick me"),
            eid="header",
        ),
        Tower(Text.lorem(), eid="body", group="center"),
        Row(
            Button("Ctrl-C : Quit"),
            Button("F12 : Screenshot"),
            eid="footer",
        ),
    ),
    rules="""
    Tower#body:
        frame: [null, heavy, null, heavy]

    Row#header, Row#footer:
        alignment: [center, start]
        height: 1
""",
)
```

Here, specifying a static dimension for both header and footer's height
allows the body to fill up the remaining space, and not specifying a width
for either (leaving it to the default `None`) makes them take up the entire
width.

**Exhibit B: N*K grid**

By making use of the `fill` dimension type, we can create responsive grids
that work regardless of the amount of widgets we have in each column / row.

```python
rows, cols = [10] * 2
grid = Tower(eid="grid")

for _ in range(rows):
    grid += Row(*[Button("Grid Item") for _ in range(cols)])

app += Page(
    grid,
    rules="""
    Tower#grid *> Button:
        width: null
        height: null

        # ...or, you could apply the pre-defined `fill` group to the widget
        groups: [fill]
""",
)
```

![rule](https://singlecolorimage.com/get/afe1af/1600x3)

### Documentation

Once the library gets to a settled state (near 1.0), documentation will be
hosted both online and as a `celx` application. Until then peep the `examples`
folder, or check out some of the widget references by using `python3 -m pydoc <name>`.

![rule](https://singlecolorimage.com/get/afe1af/1600x3)

### See also

- [Slate](https://github.com/shade40/slate): The terminal interfacing library that
    forms much of `Celadon`'s backend.
- [Zenith](https://github.com/shade40/zenith): The markup language & palette generator used
    for styling content in `Celadon`.
- [celx](https://github.com/shade40/celx): A hypermedia-driven TUI application framework
    written on top of `Celadon`.

modules.name: celadon.application
documentation: pylint: disable=too-many-lines

functions.name: celadon.application.load_rules
documentation:
Loads a set of rule declarations from YAML.

Nested get inserted into the parent's key, so:

```yaml
Button:
    height: 1
    frame: lightvertical

    /idle:
        content_style: primary-1
```

would become:

```json
{
    "Button": {
        "height": 1,
        "frame": "lightvertical",
    },
    "Button/idle": {
        "content_style": "primary-1",
    }
}
```

You can customize where the insertion takes place by marking it with an `&`. For
example:

```yaml
.fill:
    height: 1

    Progressbar&:
        height: 3
```

becomes:

```json
{
    ".fill": {
        "height": 1,
    },
    "Progressbar.fill": {
        "height": 3,
    }
}
```

Returns:
    A flattened dictionary of `{selector: {rules...}}`.

methods.name: celadon.application._TerminalWrapper.groups
documentation: Returns the current color space.

methods.name: celadon.application._TerminalWrapper.query_changed
documentation: Our query (currently) never changes at runtime.

methods.name: celadon.application._TerminalWrapper.update
documentation: No-op

classes.name: celadon.application.Selector

methods.name: celadon.application.Selector.parse
documentation:
Parses a query into a selector.

Args:
    query: The query to parse.
    direct_parent: Direct parent to test for, obtained by parsing
        `direct_parent > query`.
    indirect_parent: Direct parent to test for, obtained by parsing
        `indirect_parent *> query`.
    forced_score: A score to use when a selector matches the rule, instead of the
        score the selector calculates.

Returns:
    The obtained selector.

methods.name: celadon.application.Selector._match_parent
documentation:
Walks up the widget's parent tree and tries to match each.

Args:
    widget: The widget whos parents we are looking at.
    direct: If set, we will only walk up for one node, and use `direct_parent`
        instead of `indirect_parent`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Selector.matches
documentation:
Determines how well this selector matches the widget.

Returns:
    A score calculated as:

        (
            (eid_matches * 1000)
            + (group_matches * 500 + len(groups) * 100)
            + (state_matches * 250)
            + (type_matches * 100)
        )

    When optional parts are ommitted (like groups), they are excluded from the
    calculation. However, when these are provided the widget _must_ have
    matching attributes, otherwise the function will return 0.
TODO: We should have a Protocol to describe common actions between these classes.

The object used to query widgets.

Basic syntax is:

    WidgetType#id.group1.group2/state1|state2

...where `WidgetType` is necessary if an id is given, everything else is optional.

You can also test for hierarchy, e.g.:

    # Direct hierarchy (ParentType > WidgetType.class1)
    ParentType > WidgetType.class1

    # Indirect hierarchy (ParentType > OtherParent > WidgetType.class1)
    ParentType *> WidgetType.class1

Finally, you can use `*` to indicate 'any' in hierarchal matches:

    # WidgetType#id that is the child of any widget (all of them, so don't do this)
    * > WidgetType#id

    # Any widget whos direct parent is WidgetType#id
    WidgetType#id > *

and sets its parent to self.

documentation:
Applies the builder function.

Might be removed in the future.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.append
documentation:
Initializes & adds a widget to the Page.

Analogous to `list.append`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.extend
documentation:
Extends the page by the given widgets.

Analogous to `list.extend`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.insert
documentation:
Inserts a widget into the page.

Analogous to `list.insert`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.remove
documentation:
Removes a widget from the page.

Analogous to `list.remove`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.pop
documentation:
Pops a widget from the page.

Analogous to `list.pop`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.clear
documentation:
Removes all widgets.

Analogous to `list.clear`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.update
documentation:
Clears all widgets then adds everything given.

Analogous to `list.update`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.load_rules
documentation:
Loads the given YAML rules.

Args:
    rules: The YAML to load.
    score: A score to use when a selector matches the rule, instead of the score
        the selector calculates.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.apply_rules
documentation:
Applies the page's rules to the widgets.

Returns:
    Whether any rules were applied, e.g. whether there was any change.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.find_all
documentation: Finds all widgets in the page matching the given query.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.find
documentation: Finds the first widget in the page matching the given query.

methods.name: celadon.application.Page.rule
documentation:
Creates a new rule that matches query, and applies rules on matches.

Args:
    query: The query that will be matched against.
    score: A score to use when a selector matches the rule, instead of the score
        the selector calculates.
    rules: The rules to apply to every matching widget.

Returns:
    The selector used for matching.

A Page of an application.

It contains some children, and a set of rules it applies to them.

Initializes the Application.

Args:

Args:

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.clear_timeout
documentation:
Clears a previously added timeout.

Args:

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.append
documentation: Adds a page.

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.pin
documentation:
Pins a widget to the application.

Pinning means the app will always be displayed & on top, regardless of the
current page.

Analogous to `Page.append(self, widget)`.

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.route
documentation: Routes to a new page.

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.process_input
documentation:
Processes input.

Returns:
    Whether the input could be handled.

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.run
documentation:
Runs the application.

This function will block until the application halts.

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.pause
documentation: Pauses the app.
TODO: Expand this to clear screen (wait for frame to finish)

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.resume
documentation: Resumes the app.

methods.name: celadon.application.Application.stop
documentation: Stops the application.

class_variables.name: celadon.application.Application.children
documentation: For constantly visible, overlay-type widgets

A page to rule them all.

The primary interface to run Celadon applications. It maintains a list of pages,
between which it can navigate using routing. Only one page can be displayed at a
time, but it is possible to create constant overlays by adding widgets directly to
the Application instance, instead of a page.

classes.name: celadon.state_machine.StateMachine

methods.name: celadon.state_machine.StateMachine.__call__
documentation: Returns the current state, including substate.

methods.name: celadon.state_machine.StateMachine.copy
documentation:
Creates a copy of the state machine and modifies it according to the args.

Args:
    states: Overwrites the original machine's states.
    add_states: Adds to the original machine's states.
    transitions: Overwrites the original machine's transitions.
    add_transitions: Adds to the original machine's transitions.

methods.name: celadon.state_machine.StateMachine.apply_action
documentation:
Applies some action to the state manager.

Actions are strings that are used for updating state.

Returns:
    Whether the given action resulted in a state change.

Args:
    action: An action name.

class_variables.name: celadon.state_machine.StateMachine.on_change
documentation:
Called when the state changes.

Args:
    state: The new (changed-to) state.
A state manager to manage widget state.

functions.name: celadon.widgets.container._align

Initializes a container.

Args:

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.visible_children
documentation: Returns all children without the 'hidden' group set.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.direction
documentation: Returns and sets the current flow direction.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.direction
documentation: Sets the direction setting.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.__iadd__
documentation:
Adds a widget to the Container.

Analogous to `.append(other)`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container._is_fill
documentation:
Determines whether the widget is fill on the flow axis.

Args:
    widget: The widget to test.
    horizontal: If set, flow direction is horizontal and we return
        `widget.is_fill_width()`, instead of `is_fill_height`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container._compute_alignment_offsets
documentation:
Computes x & y offsets to align by, based on alignment and flow direction.

Args:
    child: The widget we are aligning. Used to get `computed_width` and
        `computed_height` as needed.
    available_width: The width the widget can be aligned within.
    available_height: The height the widget can be aligned within.
    horizontal: Whether flow direction is horizontal (True) or vertical (False).

Returns:
    The x offset, y offset and the modulo of the operation that got either x
        offset or y offset, based on `horizontal`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container._compute_shrink_width
documentation: TODO: Revisit shrink calculations
They either add or remove extra space (==frame.dimension), but always the wrong
one of the two.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.insert
documentation:
Inserts a widget.

Analogous to `list.insert`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.append
documentation:
Adds a new widget setting its parent attribute to self.

Analogous to `list.append`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.extend
documentation:
Extends our children by the given iterable.

Analogous to `list.extend`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.remove
documentation:
Removes a widget from self, resetting its parent attribute to None.

Analogous to `list.remove`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.pop
documentation:
Pops a widget from our children.

Analogous to `list.pop`.

Returns:
    The widget that was just removed.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.clear
documentation:
Removes all widgets.

Analogous to `list.clear`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.update_children
documentation:
Updates our children to the given iterable.

Analogous to `list.update`, doesn't use `update` because it is already a widget
method.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.replace
documentation: Replaces `current` in children to `new`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.move_by
documentation: Moves the widget (and all its children) to the given position.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.arrange
documentation:
Arranges the widget's children according to its flow.

Args:
    x: The origin's horizontal coordinate.
    y: The origin's vertical coordinate.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.container.Container.get_content
documentation: Calls our `arrange` method and returns a single empty line.

A widget that displays others based on some arrangement algorithm.

See `Tower` and `Splitter` for the implementations you should use.

modules.name: celadon.widgets.widget
documentation: pylint: disable=too-many-lines

functions.name: celadon.widgets.widget._overflows
documentation: Determines whether the given real and virtual dimensions overflow.

functions.name: celadon.widgets.widget.to_widget_space
documentation:
Computes a position as an offset from the widget's origin.

This doesn't account for positions _outside_ of a widget.

functions.name: celadon.widgets.widget.wrap_callback
documentation:
Creates a wrapper for empty widget callbacks.

This lets us maintain the callback's __name__, as opposed to doing
`lambda _: callback()`

functions.name: celadon.widgets.widget.handle_mouse_on_children
documentation:
Handles the given mouse event on an iterable of widgets.

This can be used by 'container' type widgets (like `Container`), or meta-level
management objects (like `Application`) for consistent mouse handling behaviour.

Args:
    action: The action associated with the event.
    position: The position associated with the event.
    mouse_target: The caller's current mouse target.
    hover_target: The caller's current hover target.
    children: The widgets to execute upon.

Returns:
    A tuple of:

        (success, mouse_target, hover_target)

    The input state changes based on the following rules:

    - Success is False _only_ if no child contains the given position.
    - Mouse target is...
        - Set to None on release events, or
        - Untouched if the event was not a form of "click" and the current mouse
          target could handle it, or
        - Untouched if the event was captured by another child but it could not
          handle it, or
        - Set to the first child that contained & handled the event.
    - Hover target is...
        - Set to None on release events, or
        - Set to None if a hover event is done outside the current hover target, or
        - Set to the first child that contained & handled a hover event,
        - Untouched otherwise.

classes.name: celadon.widgets.widget.BoundStyle
documentation: A callable object containing both style and fill markup.

classes.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a Widget.

Args:
    eid: The id for this widget. Defaults to a UUID.
    group: If set, `groups` is overwritten as `(group,)`.
    groups: The initial groups this widget will belong to.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.terminal
documentation: Returns the app's terminal.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.state
documentation: Returns the current state of the widget.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.styles
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of current state's style keys to bound styles.

Note that the `fill` style is inserted into every other style, and is
stored under the `_fill` key for special circumstances where you may
need to reference it.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.selected
documentation: Returns the currently selected widget.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.frame
documentation: Returns and sets the current frame.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.frame

type.name: str | tuple[str, str, str, str] | Type[Frame]

documentation: Sets the frame setting.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.alignment
documentation:
Returns and sets the current alignment settings.

Args:
    new: A tuple of either two strings (start, center, end) or two alignment
        constants.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.alignment
documentation: Sets the alignment setting.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.overflow
documentation:
Returns and sets the current overflow settings.

Args:
    new: A tuple of either two strings (start, center, end) or two alignment
        constants.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.overflow
documentation: Sets the overflow setting.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.anchor
documentation: Returns and sets the widget's anchor.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.anchor
documentation: Sets the widget's anchor.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._framed_width
documentation: Gets the widget's width excluding its frame.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._framed_height
documentation: Gets the widget's height excluding its frame.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.disabled
documentation: Returns the widget's disabled state.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.disabled
documentation: Sets the widget's disabled state.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.clipped_position
documentation: Returns the current position offset by the current clip start position.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.inner_rect
documentation: Returns the inner rect of this widget.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.outer_rect
documentation: Returns the outer rect of this widget.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._parse_markup
documentation:
Parses some markup into a span of tuples.

This also handles (ignores) zenith's FULL_RESET spans, and replaces `/`
unsetters with `/ {fill_style} {content_style}`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._apply_frame
documentation: Adds frame characters around the given lines.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._horizontal_align
documentation: Aligns a tuple of spans horizontally, using `self.alignment[0]`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._vertical_align
documentation:
Aligns a list of tuples of spans vertically, using `self.alignment[1]`.

Note that this mutates `lines`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._slice_line
documentation: Slices a line into the given width.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._apply_mouse_state
documentation: Applies a state change action based on mouse input.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._compute_shrink_width
documentation:
Computes the minimum width this widget's content takes up.

Used for `width = -1`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget._compute_shrink_height
documentation:
Computes the minimum height this widget's content takes up.

Used for `height = -1`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.setup
documentation: Use this to do simple setup actions without overriding __init__.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.serialize
documentation: Returns a dictionary to represent the dynamic value of the widget.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.update
documentation:
Updates a widget with new setttings.

Args:
    attrs: A dictionary of the widget's fields to values. Fields starting with
        `_` are disallowed, as are fields the widget doesn't already have.
    style_map: A dictionary of style keys to markup that gets merged onto our
        `style_map`'s current state, i.e.:

            `self.style_map | {self.state: style_map}`

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.as_query
documentation:
Returns the widget as the most specific selectable query.

Args:
    state: Include state suffix onto the query.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.query_changed
documentation: Returns whether the result of `as_query` has changed since last call.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.is_fill_width
documentation: Shorthand for `width is None`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.is_fill_height
documentation: Shorthand for `height is None`.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.scroll_to
documentation:
Scrolls the widget.

Both arguments may be given in one of four forms:

- `0`: scroll to the start of the widget on the given axis
- `-1`: scroll to the end of the widget on the given axis
- `0<` : scroll to the offset on the given axis
- `None`: do nothing for the given axis.

Args:
    x: The x scroll value.
    y: The y scroll value.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.has_scrollbar
documentation:
Returns whether the given dimension should display a scrollbar.

Args:
    index: The axis to check. 0 for horizontal scrolling, 1 for vertical.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.drawables
documentation: Yields all contained widgets that should be drawn.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.clip
documentation: Sets the clipping rectangle's coordinates.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.add_group
documentation: Adds a group to the widget's groups.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.remove_group
documentation: Removes a group from the widget's groups.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.toggle_group
documentation: Toggles a group in the widget's groups.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.hide
documentation: Adds the `hidden` group onto the widget.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.show
documentation: Remove the `hidden` group from the widget.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.contains
documentation: Determines whether this widget contains the given position.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.move_to
documentation: Moves the widget to the given position.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.move_by
documentation: Moves the widget by the given changes.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.remove_from_parent
documentation: Removes this widget from its parent.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.bind
documentation:
Binds a key to the given callback.

If a binding already exists, the given callback is added to the Event.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.unbind
documentation: Removes all bindings for a given key.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.handle_keyboard
documentation:
Handles a keyboard event.

Returns:
    Whether the event should continue to bubble upwards. Return `True` if the
    event was already handled.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.handle_mouse
documentation:
Handles a mouse event.

Returns:
    Whether the event should continue to bubble upwards. Return `True` if the
    event was already handled.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.compute_dimensions
documentation: Computes width & height based on our specifications and the given space.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.get_content
documentation: Gets the dynamic content for this widget.

methods.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.build
documentation: Builds the strings that represent the widget.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.width
documentation: The hint used by the widget to calculate it's width. See dimension hints.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.height
documentation: The hint used by the widget to calculate it's height. See dimension hints.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.computed_width
documentation: The current width of the widget, calculated from `width`'s hint.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.computed_height
documentation: The current height of the widget, calculated from `height`'s hint.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.scroll
documentation: The widget's (horizontal, vertical) scrolling offset.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.position
documentation: The widget's (horizontal, vertical) position.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.app
documentation: The most recently run Application instance.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.state_machine
documentation:
The state machine keeps track of the current state of the widget.

It has a tuple of possible states, and a mapping of:

    {current_state: {action: resulting_state, ...}

...that are used for state-transitions.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.style_map
documentation: The style map is the lookup table for the widget's styles at certain states.

class_variables.name: celadon.widgets.widget.Widget.rules
documentation: YAML rules for this widget, applied only when added as part of an Application.
This is a docstring.
